channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Independent review completed into police restraint death of Aboriginal man in Alice Springs
item.description: Nearly a year after the death of 24‑year‑old Aboriginal man Kumanjayi White, an external police review into the use of force by two Northern Territory officers who restrained him has concluded. Mr Whi...

item.title: Amapiano Artists Thatohatsi and Tracy Recovering After Car Accident
item.description: Popular amapiano artists Thatohatsi and Tracy, along with members of their road team, were involved in a car accident earlier this Sunday. The incident included driver Thabiso, DJ Seun1401 and road ma...

item.title: US military carries out daring rescue of airman from Iranian territory
item.description: A tense and complex US rescue mission took place after an F15E Strike Eagle fighter jet was shot down by Iranian air defences over Isfahan province. The pilot was rescued, but the second crew member, ...

item.title: NTR police initiate 30-day campaign to combat drug smuggling and addiction
item.description: In an effort to combat the rising drug menace, the NTR Commissionerate police launched the 'Drugs Pai Dandayatra 2.0' on April 6, 2026. The program aims to prevent the smuggling and sale of drugs and ...

item.title: Pretoria Police Captain Suspended Over Alleged Misappropriation of SAPS Travel Funds
item.description: A Pretoria-based police captain in the South African Police Service (SAPS) Protection Security Services has been suspended following allegations of misappropriating millions of rand intended for trave...

item.title: Balancing Protection and Freedom: How Fencing Shapes Africa’s Wildlife Parks
item.description: This article talk about one major debate wey dey happen for wildlife conservation across Africa — whether national parks suppose dey fenced or make dem open. The researchers study 60 parks across 17 A...

item.title: Study Shows Growth in Upper Middle-Class Incomes Over Decades
item.description: Recent research indicates that a larger proportion of Americans are now earning upper-middle-class incomes compared to previous decades. According to a report by the American Enterprise Institute, rou...

item.title: Raghav Chadha shares a reflective message amid internal tensions in AAP
item.description: The NDTV article, titled 'Never Outshine The Master: Raghav Chadha’s Cryptic Post Amid AAP Row', reports on a social media post shared by Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) leader Raghav Chadha during a period of ...

item.title: AAP’s Kerala Entry and Its Implications for Congress Ahead of Assembly Polls
item.description: The Aam Aadmi Party’s (AAP) decision to contest all 140 assembly seats in Kerala has stirred debate within the opposition front, exposing cracks in the INDIA alliance. Led by Arvind Kejriwal, AAP seek...

item.title: Missing Page Error on National Post Article About Canadian Travel to New York
item.description: The provided webpage from the National Post titled 'Canadian travel to New York down 2025' currently displays a 404 'Missing page' error message rather than the actual news content. It appears that th...

item.title: Former Kursk Governor Sentenced to 14 Years for Bribery in Fortification Contracts
item.description: For dem wey dey follow Russia news, one former governor of Kursk region, Alexei Smirnov, don land for jail for 14 years after e confess say e dey collect bribe. E and two associates collect over $250,...

item.title: Understanding coded language used by online child predators and how parents can protect their children
item.description: The article examines how children and teenagers are increasingly exposed to coded or euphemistic online language used by adults with harmful intentions. It recounts the author’s discovery of the term ...

item.title: Tanzania to enforce energy efficiency standards on electrical appliances from July 2026
item.description: Tanzania iko set to start restricting importation and distribution of electrical appliances wey consume too much power from July 2026. Government wan make sure only energy-efficient products enter the...

item.title: The Alibi: Stella Vows Support for Katherine and Joseph in Latest Episode
item.description: In the latest episode of 'The Alibi,' Stella made it clear she won’t be leaving Katherine and Joseph to face their challenges alone. The episode, aired on April 07, 2026, focused on Stella's commitmen...

item.title: Rising Fuel Prices Could Increase Driving Lesson Costs and Test Delays
item.description: The cost of learning to drive in the UK is under pressure as fuel prices surge following the conflict in Iran. Driving instructors, such as Rachael Hutson-Lumb in Shropshire, have already increased le...

item.title: Astrocytes Play a Key Role in Forming and Regulating Fear Memories
item.description: Recent research from the University of Arizona has revealed that astrocytes, brain cells previously considered mere support elements, are crucial in shaping fear memories. In a multi-institutional stu...

item.title: Sydney Morning Herald Superquiz – April 7, 2026 Edition
item.description: The article features a Superquiz, a popular quiz that appears regularly in The Sydney Morning Herald. It provides readers with a set of questions that test their general knowledge. The quiz is dated f...

item.title: Gauteng Premier Confirms Completion of Brixton Reservoir to Strengthen Johannesburg Water Security
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi has confirmed the successful completion of a new ground reservoir and tower in Brixton, Johannesburg, alongside an emergency boosting pumping station. These infrastructu...

item.title: Dar Swim Club prepares intensely for national swimming championships and World Aquatics qualifiers
item.description: Swimmers from Dar es Salaam Swim Club are in final preparations for the Tanzania National Club Championships, set to take place on April 11 and 12 at the International School of Tanganyika (IST) pool ...

item.title: Impact of Hormuz Strait closure on Middle Eastern oil economies
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial shipping route for oil, has drastically affected Middle Eastern economies. The conflict between Iran, the U.S., and Israel has caused massive disruptions...
